nameMeanings.name: Hilton
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: habitational name from any of various places called Hilton (e.g. in Cambridgeshire, Derbyshire, Staffordshire, North Yorkshire, and Westmorland), most of which derive from Old English hyll ‘hill’ + tūn ‘farmstead, estate’, though some come from Old English helde ‘slope’ + tūn.

occasionally, as in the case of Ralph filius Hilton (Yorkshire 1219), perhaps from the personal name Hilton, itself a possible form of Norman Hildun, composed of the ancient Germanic elements hild ‘strife, battle’ + hūn ‘bear cub’. Compare Hilt . This English surname is present in Ireland (mainly taken to Ulster in the early 17th century, though recorded earlier in Dublin).

variants.name: Hulton
meanings: English (Lancashire): habitational name from Hulton (Lancashire) or Hulton (Staffordshire), both from Old English hyll ‘hill’ + tūn ‘farmstead, estate’. Compare Hilton .

variants.name: Hilt
meanings: German: from a short form of Hildebrand or some other compound ancient Germanic name formed with hild ‘strife, battle’ as the first element. See Hild .
English (London): from the Middle English personal name Hilte, ultimately from ancient Germanic Helto, probably from halith- ‘hero’.

nameMeanings.name: Robert
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the many French names of Germanic origin that were introduced into Britain by the Normans; it has since remained in continuous use. It is derived from the nearly synonymous elements hrōd ‘fame’ + berht ‘bright, famous’, and had a native Old English predecessor of similar form (Hreodbeorht), which was supplanted by the Norman name. Two dukes of Normandy in the 11th century bore the name: the father of William the Conqueror (sometimes identified with the legendary Robert the Devil), and his eldest son. It was borne also by three kings of Scotland, notably Robert the Bruce ( 1274–1329 ), who freed Scotland from English domination. The altered short form Bob is very common, but Hob and Dob, which were common in the Middle Ages and gave rise to surnames, are extinct. See also Rupert .

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir Robert Hilton VI Baron was born in 1350, in Swine, Yorkshire, England, his father, Robert de Hilton III, was 29 and his mother, Matilda de Campania, was 25. He married Constance de Sutton in 1390, in Yorkshire, England. They were the parents of at least 1 son. He died in 1410, in Swine, Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 60, and was buried in Swine, Yorkshire East Riding, England, United Kingdom.

Il a épousé Constance de Sutton en 1390, à Everwyck, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils. Il est décédé en 1410, à Swine, Everwyck, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 60 ans, et a été enterré à Swine, Yorkshire East Riding, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

ko.Swine, Yorkshire East Riding, England, U: 영국 잉글랜드 요르크실르 이스트 리딩 스윈
ko.lifeSummary: Sir Robert Hilton VI Baron(이)가 1350년 영국 요르크실르 스윈에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Robert de Hilton III(은)는 29세였고, 그의 어머니 Matilda de Campania(은)는 25세였다. 그는 1390년 영국 요르크실르에서 Constance de Sutton(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1410년 영국 잉글랜드 요르크실르 스윈에서 60세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 요르크실르 이스트 리딩 스윈에 묻혔다.

Он женился на женщине по имени Constance de Sutton в 1390 г. в местности еркшир, Англия. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын. Он умер в 1410 г. в местности сваин, еркшир, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 60 лет и был похоронен в местности сваин, еркшир эаст ридинг, Англия, Великобритания.
